title: bonannia resinifera
intro: Bonannia resinifera, also known as Bonna or Mexican Bonana, is a plant species belonging to the Sapindaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and fruits have been used in herbal preparations to promote digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Bonannia resinifera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its health benefits.
